entries.detail:
1â†’# Specification: Fix Git Merge & Configure Deepgram SDK
     2â†’
     3â†’## Overview
     4â†’
     5â†’This specification covers a two-phase critical task: (1) resolving a Git merge conflict where the current branch has no common history with `main`, requiring a force merge using `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` via terminal; and (2) implementing Epic 2: Connection & Configuration for a Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK integration in a greenfield voice AI assistant project. The project will use Python with the Deepgram SDK to establish real-time voice WebSocket connections.
     6â†’
     7â†’## Workflow Type
     8â†’
     9â†’**Type**: feature
    10â†’
    11â†’**Rationale**: This involves creating new functionality (Deepgram SDK integration) combined with a prerequisite infrastructure fix (Git merge). The task requires implementing new features rather than refactoring existing code or investigating issues.
    12â†’
    13â†’## Task Scope
    14â†’
    15â†’### Services Involved
    16â†’- **Voice Agent Backend** (primary/new) - Python service using Deepgram SDK for real-time voice processing
    17â†’- **Frontend Client** (future integration) - Will connect to backend for audio streaming (out of scope for this task)
    18â†’
    19â†’### This Task Will:
    20â†’- [ ] Execute `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` to force merge current branch into main via terminal
    21â†’- [ ] Resolve any merge conflicts that arise during the merge process
    22â†’- [ ] Install Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` Python package)
    23â†’- [ ] Create initial project structure (`/backend` directory with Python files)
    24â†’- [ ] Configure SDK settings with environment variables
    25â†’- [ ] Implement `keepAlive()` function to send KeepAlive control messages every 8 seconds
    26â†’- [ ] Establish WebSocket connection pattern using context manager
    27â†’
    28â†’### Out of Scope:
    29â†’- Frontend implementation (Epic 3)
    30â†’- Audio streaming and playback (Epic 3)
    31â†’- Interruption/barge-in logic (Epic 4)
    32â†’- SaaS features (auth, credits, Stripe) (Epic 5)
    33â†’- Deployment to Vercel/Render (Epic 6)
    34â†’
    35â†’## Service Context
    36â†’
    37â†’### Voice Agent Backend (New Service)
    38â†’
    39â†’**Tech Stack:**
    40â†’- Language: Python 3.10+
    41â†’- Framework: Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` v5.x)
    42â†’- Key directories: `/backend` (to be created)
    43â†’
    44â†’**Entry Point:** `backend/main.py` (to be created)
    45â†’
    46â†’**How to Run:**
    47â†’```bash
    48â†’# Install dependencies
    49â†’pip install deepgram-sdk python-dotenv
    50â†’
    51â†’# Run the application
    52â†’python backend/main.py
    53â†’```
    54â†’
    55â†’**Port:** TBD (not yet defined, will be determined during implementation)
    56â†’
    57â†’## Files to Create
    58â†’
    59â†’| File | Service | Purpose |
    60â†’|------|---------|---------|
    61â†’| `backend/main.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Main entry point with Deepgram connection setup |
    62â†’| `backend/requirements.txt` | Voice Agent Backend | Python dependencies (deepgram-sdk, python-dotenv) |
    63â†’| `backend/.env.example` | Voice Agent Backend | Environment variable template |
    64â†’| `backend/agent.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Agent configuration and keepAlive implementation |
    65â†’
    66â†’## Files to Reference
    67â†’
    68â†’Note: This is a greenfield project with no existing files to reference. The following patterns are derived from research and Deepgram SDK documentation:
    69â†’
    70â†’| Pattern | Source | What to Follow |
    71â†’|---------|--------|----------------|
    72â†’| Context manager pattern | Deepgram SDK docs | Use `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:` for automatic cleanup |
    73â†’| KeepAlive timing | Research findings (v5.x breaking change) | Send KeepAlive control message every 8 seconds, not 5 |
    74â†’| Event handler registration | Deepgram SDK docs | Register all handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()` |
    75â†’
    76â†’## Patterns to Follow
    77â†’
    78â†’### Deepgram Agent Connection Pattern
    79â†’
    80â†’From Deepgram SDK documentation:
    81â†’
    82â†’```python
    83â†’from deepgram import DeepgramClient
    84â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import (
    85â†’    AgentV1SettingsMessage, AgentV1ControlMessage, AgentV1MediaMessage
    86â†’)
    87â†’
    88â†’# Required: context manager pattern for automatic cleanup
    89â†’with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
    90â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    91â†’    connection.start_listening()
    92â†’```
    93â†’
    94â†’**Key Points:**
    95â†’- Context manager ensures proper connection cleanup on exit
    96â†’- Must send settings immediately after connection opens
    97â†’- `start_listening()` begins the event loop
    98â†’
    99â†’### KeepAlive Pattern (Critical - v5.x Breaking Change)
   100â†’
   101â†’```python
   102â†’import time
   103â†’
   104â†’def keep_alive_loop(connection):
   105â†’    """Send KeepAlive every 8 seconds during silence."""
   106â†’    while connection.is_connected:
   107â†’        connection.send_control(AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive'))
   108â†’        time.sleep(8)
   109â†’```
   110â†’
   111â†’**Key Points:**
   112â†’- KeepAlive is NOT automatic in v5.x (major breaking change)
   113â†’- Must be manually triggered every 8 seconds during silence periods
   114â†’- Server sends NO response to KeepAlive messages
   115â†’- Required to prevent WebSocket timeout during audio gaps
   116â†’
   117â†’### Event Handler Registration Pattern
   118â†’
   119â†’```python
   120â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
   121â†’    """Send settings when connection opens."""
   122â†’    settings = AgentV1SettingsMessage(
   123â†’        listen=AgentV1ListenSettings(model="nova-3"),
   124â†’        think=AgentV1ThinkSettings(provider="openai", model="gpt-4o-mini"),
   125â†’        speak=AgentV1SpeakSettings(voice="aura-2-thalia-en")
   126â†’    )
   127â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
   128â†’
   129â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
   130â†’    """Handle both JSON messages AND binary audio separately."""
   131â†’    # Check for message type
   132â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
   133â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
   134â†’        # JSON event handling
   135â†’        pass
   136â†’    else:
   137â†’        # Binary audio handling
   138â†’        pass
   139â†’
   140â†’# Register BEFORE start_listening()
   141â†’connection.on(LiveEvents.OPEN, on_open)
   142â†’connection.on(LiveEvents.MESSAGE, on_message)
   143â†’connection.start_listening()
   144â†’```
   145â†’
   146â†’**Key Points:**
   147â†’- Event handlers MUST be registered before `start_listening()`
   148â†’- `MESSAGE` event can receive both JSON and binary data
   149â†’- `OPEN` event is where settings should be sent
   150â†’
   151â†’## Requirements
   152â†’
   153â†’### Functional Requirements
   154â†’
   155â†’1. **Git Merge Resolution**
   156â†’   - Description: Force merge current branch into main using terminal Git commands
   157â†’   - Acceptance: Merge completes successfully, `git log` shows merged commits
   158â†’
   159â†’2. **Deepgram SDK Installation**
   160â†’   - Description: Install `deepgram-sdk` v5.x and `python-dotenv` packages
   161â†’   - Acceptance: `pip show deepgram-sdk` shows installed package
   162â†’
   163â†’3. **Project Structure**
   164â†’   - Description: Create `/backend` directory with initial Python files
   165â†’   - Acceptance: Directory exists with `main.py`, `requirements.txt`, `.env.example`, `agent.py`
   166â†’
   167â†’4. **Environment Configuration**
   168â†’   - Description: Configure DEEPGRAM_API_KEY and optionally OPENAI_API_KEY
   169â†’   - Acceptance: `.env` file exists with required variables, `.env.example` documents them
   170â†’
   171â†’5. **WebSocket Connection**
   172â†’   - Description: Establish connection using context manager pattern
   173â†’   - Acceptance: Connection opens successfully, OPEN event fires
   174â†’
   175â†’6. **Agent Settings Configuration**
   176â†’   - Description: Configure Listen (nova-3), Think (gpt-4o-mini), Speak (aura-2-thalia-en)
   177â†’   - Acceptance: Settings sent successfully after connection opens
   178â†’
   179â†’7. **KeepAlive Function**
   180â†’   - Description: Implement keepAlive() sending control message every 8 seconds
   181â†’   - Acceptance: Function sends `AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive')` every 8s during silence
   182â†’
   183â†’### Edge Cases
   184â†’
   185â†’1. **Git Merge Conflicts** - Resolve any conflicts manually, preserving both histories where possible
   186â†’2. **Missing API Keys** - Raise clear error message if DEEPGRAM_API_KEY not set
   187â†’3. **Connection Failure** - Implement retry logic or graceful error handling
   188â†’4. **KeepAlive During Audio** - Only send KeepAlive during silence periods, not during active transmission
   189â†’5. **WebSocket Disconnect** - Handle CLOSE and ERROR events with cleanup
   190â†’
   191â†’## Implementation Notes
   192â†’
   193â†’### DO
   194â†’- Use terminal for Git merge: `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories <branch-name>`
   195â†’- Create `/backend` directory structure following Python best practices
   196â†’- Use context manager pattern: `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:`
   197â†’- Send KeepAlive every 8 seconds (NOT 5 - plan.md has outdated info)
   198â†’- Register event handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()`
   199â†’- Use python-dotenv for environment variable management
   200â†’- Include `.env.example` in version control (not `.env`)
   201â†’
   202â†’### DON'T
   203â†’- Do NOT use GitHub PR web interface for the merge (will fail with unrelated histories)
   204â†’- Do NOT send KeepAlive every 5 seconds (research confirms 8 seconds is correct)
   205â†’- Do NOT call `start_listening()` before registering event handlers
   206â†’- Do NOT commit `.env` file with actual API keys
   207â†’- Do NOT implement frontend features (Epic 3) in this task
   208â†’
   209â†’### Git Merge Specific Steps
   210â†’
   211â†’```bash
   212â†’# 1. Check current branch
   213â†’git branch
   214â†’
   215â†’# 2. Switch to main branch
   216â†’git checkout main
   217â†’
   218â†’# 3. Pull latest main (if remote exists)
   219â†’git pull origin main
   220â†’
   221â†’# 4. Merge with unrelated histories
   222â†’git merge --allow-unrelated-histories <your-branch-name>
   223â†’
   224â†’# 5. Resolve conflicts if any occur
   225â†’# Ed

1â†’"""
     2â†’Deepgram Voice Agent Main Entry Point
     3â†’
     4â†’This module initializes the Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK with environment
     5â†’configuration and establishes WebSocket connections for real-time voice processing.
     6â†’"""
     7â†’
     8â†’import os
     9â†’from dotenv import load_dotenv
    10â†’from deepgram import DeepgramClient
    11â†’from deepgram.core.events import EventType
    12â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import AgentV1ControlMessage
    13â†’
    14â†’from agent import get_agent_settings
    15â†’
    16â†’
    17â†’def initialize_deepgram_client():
    18â†’    """
    19â†’    Initialize and return a DeepgramClient instance with API key from environment.
    20â†’
    21â†’    Returns:
    22â†’        DeepgramClient: Configured Deepgram client instance
    23â†’
    24â†’    Raises:
    25â†’        ValueError: If DEEPGRAM_API_KEY is not set in environment variables
    26â†’
    27â†’    Example:
    28â†’        >>> client = initialize_deepgram_client()
    29â†’        >>> with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
    30â†’        ...     # Handle connection
    31â†’    """
    32â†’    # Load environment variables from .env file
    33â†’    load_dotenv()
    34â†’
    35â†’    # Get API key from environment
    36â†’    deepgram_api_key = os.getenv("DEEPGRAM_API_KEY")
    37â†’
    38â†’    # Validate API key is present
    39â†’    if not deepgram_api_key:
    40â†’        raise ValueError(
    41â†’            "DEEPGRAM_API_KEY environment variable is not set. "
    42â†’            "Please set it in your .env file or environment."
    43â†’        )
    44â†’
    45â†’    # Create and return DeepgramClient instance
    46â†’    return DeepgramClient(deepgram_api_key)
    47â†’
    48â†’
    49â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
    50â†’    """
    51â†’    Handle the WebSocket OPEN event.
    52â†’
    53â†’    This event fires when the connection is established. Send agent settings
    54â†’    immediately after connection opens to configure the voice agent.
    55â†’
    56â†’    Args:
    57â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    58â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments
    59â†’    """
    60â†’    settings = get_agent_settings()
    61â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    62â†’
    63â†’
    64â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
    65â†’    """
    66â†’    Handle the WebSocket MESSAGE event.
    67â†’
    68â†’    This event receives both JSON control messages and binary audio data.
    69â†’    Check message type to determine handling approach.
    70â†’
    71â†’    Args:
    72â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    73â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'message' which may be
    74â†’                  a dict (JSON) or bytes (binary audio)
    75â†’    """
    76â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
    77â†’
    78â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
    79â†’        # Handle JSON control messages
    80â†’        # TODO: Implement JSON message handling in future subtasks
    81â†’        pass
    82â†’    elif isinstance(message, bytes):
    83â†’        # Handle binary audio data
    84â†’        # TODO: Implement audio handling in future subtasks
    85â†’        pass
    86â†’
    87â†’
    88â†’def on_close(connection, **kwargs):
    89â†’    """
    90â†’    Handle the WebSocket CLOSE event.
    91â†’
    92â†’    This event fires when the connection is closed. Perform cleanup here.
    93â†’
    94â†’    Args:
    95â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    96â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments
    97â†’    """
    98â†’    # TODO: Implement cleanup in future subtasks
    99â†’    pass
   100â†’
   101â†’
   102â†’def on_error(connection, **kwargs):
   103â†’    """
   104â†’    Handle the WebSocket ERROR event.
   105â†’
   106â†’    This event fires when an error occurs on the connection.
   107â†’
   108â†’    Args:
   109â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
   110â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'error' with error details
   111â†’    """
   112â†’    error = kwargs.get("error")
   113â†’    # TODO: Implement error handling in future subtasks
   114â†’    pass
   115â†’
   116â†’
   117â†’def main():
   118â†’    """
   119â†’    Main entry point for the Deepgram Voice Agent application.
   120â†’
   121â†’    Establishes WebSocket connection using context manager pattern,
   122â†’    registers event handlers, and starts listening for voice events.
   123â†’    """
   124â†’    try:
   125â†’        # Initialize the Deepgram client with environment configuration
   126â†’        client = initialize_deepgram_client()
   127â†’
   128â†’        # Use context manager for automatic connection cleanup
   129â†’        with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
   130â†’            # Register event handlers BEFORE calling start_listening()
   131â†’            connection.on(EventType.OPEN, on_open)
   132â†’            connection.on(EventType.MESSAGE, on_message)
   133â†’            connection.on(EventType.CLOSE, on_close)
   134â†’            connection.on(EventType.ERROR, on_error)
   135â†’
   136â†’            # Start the event loop
   137â†’            connection.start_listening()
   138â†’
   139â†’    except ValueError as e:
   140â†’        print(f"Configuration error: {e}")
   141â†’        return 1
   142â†’    except Exception as e:
   143â†’        print(f"Unexpected error: {e}")
   144â†’        return 1
   145â†’
   146â†’    return 0
   147â†’
   148â†’
   149â†’if __name__ == "__main__":
   150â†’    exit(main())
   151â†’

 def main():

entries.content:
## Summary

I have successfully completed **subtask-4-2: Implement context manager connection pattern**.

### What was implemented:

1. **Added imports** to `backend/main.py`:
   - `AgentV1ControlMessage` from `deepgram.extensions.types.sockets`
   - `get_agent_settings` from `agent` module

2. **Implemented 4 event handlers**:
   - `on_open`: Sends `AgentV1SettingsMessage` via `get_agent_settings()` when connection opens
   - `on_message`: Handles both JSON (`dict`) and binary audio (`bytes`) messages
   - `on_close`: Cleanup handler for connection closure
   - `on_error`: Error handling for connection errors

3. **Updated `main()` function**:
   - Uses context manager pattern: `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:`
   - Registers all 4 event handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()`
   - Maintains existing error handling for `ValueError` and generic exceptions

### Verification:
- âś… Context manager pattern correctly implemented
- âś… All 4 event handlers (OPEN, MESSAGE, CLOSE, ERROR) registered
- âś… Handlers registered in correct order (before `start_listening()`)
- âś… `on_open` handler sends settings via `connection.send_settings(settings)`
- âś… No console.log/print debugging statements
- âś… Error handling in place

### Commit:
```
commit 590300c
auto-claude: subtask-4-2 - Implement context manager connection pattern
```

The implementation plan has been updated and this subtask is now marked as **completed**.
I'll start by reading the spec and implementation plan to understand what exactly needs to be implemented for this subtask.

entries.detail:
1â†’# Specification: Fix Git Merge & Configure Deepgram SDK
     2â†’
     3â†’## Overview
     4â†’
     5â†’This specification covers a two-phase critical task: (1) resolving a Git merge conflict where the current branch has no common history with `main`, requiring a force merge using `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` via terminal; and (2) implementing Epic 2: Connection & Configuration for a Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK integration in a greenfield voice AI assistant project. The project will use Python with the Deepgram SDK to establish real-time voice WebSocket connections.
     6â†’
     7â†’## Workflow Type
     8â†’
     9â†’**Type**: feature
    10â†’
    11â†’**Rationale**: This involves creating new functionality (Deepgram SDK integration) combined with a prerequisite infrastructure fix (Git merge). The task requires implementing new features rather than refactoring existing code or investigating issues.
    12â†’
    13â†’## Task Scope
    14â†’
    15â†’### Services Involved
    16â†’- **Voice Agent Backend** (primary/new) - Python service using Deepgram SDK for real-time voice processing
    17â†’- **Frontend Client** (future integration) - Will connect to backend for audio streaming (out of scope for this task)
    18â†’
    19â†’### This Task Will:
    20â†’- [ ] Execute `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` to force merge current branch into main via terminal
    21â†’- [ ] Resolve any merge conflicts that arise during the merge process
    22â†’- [ ] Install Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` Python package)
    23â†’- [ ] Create initial project structure (`/backend` directory with Python files)
    24â†’- [ ] Configure SDK settings with environment variables
    25â†’- [ ] Implement `keepAlive()` function to send KeepAlive control messages every 8 seconds
    26â†’- [ ] Establish WebSocket connection pattern using context manager
    27â†’
    28â†’### Out of Scope:
    29â†’- Frontend implementation (Epic 3)
    30â†’- Audio streaming and playback (Epic 3)
    31â†’- Interruption/barge-in logic (Epic 4)
    32â†’- SaaS features (auth, credits, Stripe) (Epic 5)
    33â†’- Deployment to Vercel/Render (Epic 6)
    34â†’
    35â†’## Service Context
    36â†’
    37â†’### Voice Agent Backend (New Service)
    38â†’
    39â†’**Tech Stack:**
    40â†’- Language: Python 3.10+
    41â†’- Framework: Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` v5.x)
    42â†’- Key directories: `/backend` (to be created)
    43â†’
    44â†’**Entry Point:** `backend/main.py` (to be created)
    45â†’
    46â†’**How to Run:**
    47â†’```bash
    48â†’# Install dependencies
    49â†’pip install deepgram-sdk python-dotenv
    50â†’
    51â†’# Run the application
    52â†’python backend/main.py
    53â†’```
    54â†’
    55â†’**Port:** TBD (not yet defined, will be determined during implementation)
    56â†’
    57â†’## Files to Create
    58â†’
    59â†’| File | Service | Purpose |
    60â†’|------|---------|---------|
    61â†’| `backend/main.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Main entry point with Deepgram connection setup |
    62â†’| `backend/requirements.txt` | Voice Agent Backend | Python dependencies (deepgram-sdk, python-dotenv) |
    63â†’| `backend/.env.example` | Voice Agent Backend | Environment variable template |
    64â†’| `backend/agent.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Agent configuration and keepAlive implementation |
    65â†’
    66â†’## Files to Reference
    67â†’
    68â†’Note: This is a greenfield project with no existing files to reference. The following patterns are derived from research and Deepgram SDK documentation:
    69â†’
    70â†’| Pattern | Source | What to Follow |
    71â†’|---------|--------|----------------|
    72â†’| Context manager pattern | Deepgram SDK docs | Use `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:` for automatic cleanup |
    73â†’| KeepAlive timing | Research findings (v5.x breaking change) | Send KeepAlive control message every 8 seconds, not 5 |
    74â†’| Event handler registration | Deepgram SDK docs | Register all handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()` |
    75â†’
    76â†’## Patterns to Follow
    77â†’
    78â†’### Deepgram Agent Connection Pattern
    79â†’
    80â†’From Deepgram SDK documentation:
    81â†’
    82â†’```python
    83â†’from deepgram import DeepgramClient
    84â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import (
    85â†’    AgentV1SettingsMessage, AgentV1ControlMessage, AgentV1MediaMessage
    86â†’)
    87â†’
    88â†’# Required: context manager pattern for automatic cleanup
    89â†’with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
    90â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    91â†’    connection.start_listening()
    92â†’```
    93â†’
    94â†’**Key Points:**
    95â†’- Context manager ensures proper connection cleanup on exit
    96â†’- Must send settings immediately after connection opens
    97â†’- `start_listening()` begins the event loop
    98â†’
    99â†’### KeepAlive Pattern (Critical - v5.x Breaking Change)
   100â†’
   101â†’```python
   102â†’import time
   103â†’
   104â†’def keep_alive_loop(connection):
   105â†’    """Send KeepAlive every 8 seconds during silence."""
   106â†’    while connection.is_connected:
   107â†’        connection.send_control(AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive'))
   108â†’        time.sleep(8)
   109â†’```
   110â†’
   111â†’**Key Points:**
   112â†’- KeepAlive is NOT automatic in v5.x (major breaking change)
   113â†’- Must be manually triggered every 8 seconds during silence periods
   114â†’- Server sends NO response to KeepAlive messages
   115â†’- Required to prevent WebSocket timeout during audio gaps
   116â†’
   117â†’### Event Handler Registration Pattern
   118â†’
   119â†’```python
   120â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
   121â†’    """Send settings when connection opens."""
   122â†’    settings = AgentV1SettingsMessage(
   123â†’        listen=AgentV1ListenSettings(model="nova-3"),
   124â†’        think=AgentV1ThinkSettings(provider="openai", model="gpt-4o-mini"),
   125â†’        speak=AgentV1SpeakSettings(voice="aura-2-thalia-en")
   126â†’    )
   127â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
   128â†’
   129â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
   130â†’    """Handle both JSON messages AND binary audio separately."""
   131â†’    # Check for message type
   132â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
   133â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
   134â†’        # JSON event handling
   135â†’        pass
   136â†’    else:
   137â†’        # Binary audio handling
   138â†’        pass
   139â†’
   140â†’# Register BEFORE start_listening()
   141â†’connection.on(LiveEvents.OPEN, on_open)
   142â†’connection.on(LiveEvents.MESSAGE, on_message)
   143â†’connection.start_listening()
   144â†’```
   145â†’
   146â†’**Key Points:**
   147â†’- Event handlers MUST be registered before `start_listening()`
   148â†’- `MESSAGE` event can receive both JSON and binary data
   149â†’- `OPEN` event is where settings should be sent
   150â†’
   151â†’## Requirements
   152â†’
   153â†’### Functional Requirements
   154â†’
   155â†’1. **Git Merge Resolution**
   156â†’   - Description: Force merge current branch into main using terminal Git commands
   157â†’   - Acceptance: Merge completes successfully, `git log` shows merged commits
   158â†’
   159â†’2. **Deepgram SDK Installation**
   160â†’   - Description: Install `deepgram-sdk` v5.x and `python-dotenv` packages
   161â†’   - Acceptance: `pip show deepgram-sdk` shows installed package
   162â†’
   163â†’3. **Project Structure**
   164â†’   - Description: Create `/backend` directory with initial Python files
   165â†’   - Acceptance: Directory exists with `main.py`, `requirements.txt`, `.env.example`, `agent.py`
   166â†’
   167â†’4. **Environment Configuration**
   168â†’   - Description: Configure DEEPGRAM_API_KEY and optionally OPENAI_API_KEY
   169â†’   - Acceptance: `.env` file exists with required variables, `.env.example` documents them
   170â†’
   171â†’5. **WebSocket Connection**
   172â†’   - Description: Establish connection using context manager pattern
   173â†’   - Acceptance: Connection opens successfully, OPEN event fires
   174â†’
   175â†’6. **Agent Settings Configuration**
   176â†’   - Description: Configure Listen (nova-3), Think (gpt-4o-mini), Speak (aura-2-thalia-en)
   177â†’   - Acceptance: Settings sent successfully after connection opens
   178â†’
   179â†’7. **KeepAlive Function**
   180â†’   - Description: Implement keepAlive() sending control message every 8 seconds
   181â†’   - Acceptance: Function sends `AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive')` every 8s during silence
   182â†’
   183â†’### Edge Cases
   184â†’
   185â†’1. **Git Merge Conflicts** - Resolve any conflicts manually, preserving both histories where possible
   186â†’2. **Missing API Keys** - Raise clear error message if DEEPGRAM_API_KEY not set
   187â†’3. **Connection Failure** - Implement retry logic or graceful error handling
   188â†’4. **KeepAlive During Audio** - Only send KeepAlive during silence periods, not during active transmission
   189â†’5. **WebSocket Disconnect** - Handle CLOSE and ERROR events with cleanup
   190â†’
   191â†’## Implementation Notes
   192â†’
   193â†’### DO
   194â†’- Use terminal for Git merge: `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories <branch-name>`
   195â†’- Create `/backend` directory structure following Python best practices
   196â†’- Use context manager pattern: `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:`

   198â†’- Register event handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()`
   199â†’- Use python-dotenv for environment variable management
   200â†’- Include `.env.example` in version control (not `.env`)
   201â†’
   202â†’### DON'T
   203â†’- Do NOT use GitHub PR web interface for the merge (will fail with unrelated histories)
   204â†’- Do NOT send KeepAlive every 5 seconds (research confirms 8 seconds is correct)
   205â†’- Do NOT call `start_listening()` before registering event handlers
   206â†’- Do NOT commit `.env` file with actual API keys
   207â†’- Do NOT implement frontend features (Epic 3) in this task
   208â†’
   209â†’### Git Merge Specific Steps
   210â†’
   211â†’```bash
   212â†’# 1. Check current branch
   213â†’git branch
   214â†’
   215â†’# 2. Switch to main branch

   223â†’
   224â†’# 5. Resolve conflicts if any occur
   225â†’# Ed

    17â†’def initialize_deepgram_client():
    18â†’    """
    19â†’    Initialize and return a DeepgramClient instance with API key from environment.
    20â†’
    21â†’    Returns:
    22â†’        DeepgramClient: Configured Deepgram client instance
    23â†’
    24â†’    Raises:
    25â†’        ValueError: If DEEPGRAM_API_KEY is not set in environment variables
    26â†’
    27â†’    Example:
    28â†’        >>> client = initialize_deepgram_client()
    29â†’        >>> with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
    30â†’        ...     # Handle connection
    31â†’    """
    32â†’    # Load environment variables from .env file
    33â†’    load_dotenv()
    34â†’
    35â†’    # Get API key from environment
    36â†’    deepgram_api_key = os.getenv("DEEPGRAM_API_KEY")
    37â†’
    38â†’    # Validate API key is present
    39â†’    if not deepgram_api_key:
    40â†’        raise ValueError(
    41â†’            "DEEPGRAM_API_KEY environment variable is not set. "
    42â†’            "Please set it in your .env file or environment."
    43â†’        )
    44â†’
    45â†’    # Create and return DeepgramClient instance
    46â†’    return DeepgramClient(deepgram_api_key)
    47â†’
    48â†’
    49â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
    50â†’    """
    51â†’    Handle the WebSocket OPEN event.
    52â†’
    53â†’    This event fires when the connection is established. Send agent settings
    54â†’    immediately after connection opens to configure the voice agent.
    55â†’
    56â†’    Args:
    57â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    58â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments
    59â†’    """
    60â†’    settings = get_agent_settings()
    61â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    62â†’
    63â†’
    64â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
    65â†’    """
    66â†’    Handle the WebSocket MESSAGE event.
    67â†’
    68â†’    This event receives both JSON control messages and binary audio data.
    69â†’    Check message type to determine handling approach.
    70â†’
    71â†’    Args:
    72â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    73â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'message' which may be
    74â†’                  a dict (JSON) or bytes (binary audio)
    75â†’    """
    76â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
    77â†’
    78â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
    79â†’        # Handle JSON control messages
    80â†’        # TODO: Implement JSON message handling in future subtasks
    81â†’        pass
    82â†’    elif isinstance(message, bytes):
    83â†’        # Handle binary audio data
    84â†’        # TODO: Implement audio handling in future subtasks
    85â†’        pass
    86â†’
    87â†’
    88â†’def on_close(connection, **kwargs):
    89â†’    """
    90â†’    Handle the WebSocket CLOSE event.
    91â†’
    92â†’    This event fires when the connection is closed. Log the closure
    93â†’    and perform any necessary cleanup.
    94â†’
    95â†’    Args:
    96â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    97â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'code' (close code)
    98â†’                  and 'reason' (close reason)
    99â†’    """
   100â†’    close_code = kwargs.get("code")
   101â†’    close_reason = kwargs.get("reason")
   102â†’
   103â†’    if close_code:
   104â†’        print(f"Connection closed: code={close_code}, reason={close_reason}")
   105â†’    else:
   106â†’        print("Connection closed")
   107â†’
   108â†’
   109â†’def on_error(connection, **kwargs):
   110â†’    """
   111â†’    Handle the WebSocket ERROR event.
   112â†’
   113â†’    This event fires when an error occurs on the connection.
   114â†’
   115â†’    Args:
   116â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
   117â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'error' with error details
   118â†’    """
   119â†’    error = kwargs.get("error")
   120â†’    if error:
   121â†’        print(f"Connection error: {error}")
   122â†’    else:
   123â†’        print("Connection error occurred")
   124â†’
   125â†’
   126â†’def main():
   127â†’    """
   128â†’    Main entry point for the Deepgram Voice Agent application.
   129â†’
   130â†’    Establishes WebSocket connection using context manager pattern,
   131â†’    registers event handlers, and starts listening for voice events.
   132â†’    """
   133â†’    try:
   134â†’        # Initialize the Deepgram client with environment configuration
   135â†’        client = initialize_deepgram_client()
   136â†’
   137â†’        # Use context manager for automatic connection cleanup
   138â†’        with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
   139â†’            # Register event handlers BEFORE calling start_listening()
   140â†’            connection.on(EventType.OPEN, on_open)
   141â†’            connection.on(EventType.MESSAGE, on_message)
   142â†’            connection.on(EventType.CLOSE, on_close)
   143â†’            connection.on(EventType.ERROR, on_error)
   144â†’
   145â†’            # Start the event loop
   146â†’            connection.start_listening()
   147â†’
   148â†’    except ValueError as e:
   149â†’        print(f"Configuration error: {e}")
   150â†’        return 1
   151â†’    except Exception as e:
   152â†’        print(f"Unexpected error: {e}")
   153â†’        return 1
   154â†’
   155â†’    return 0
   156â†’
   157â†’
   158â†’if __name__ == "__main__":
   159â†’    exit(main())
   160â†’

The implementation is complete. Here's a summary of what was done for **subtask-4-3**:

## Completed: Implement event handlers with correct registration order

### Changes Made to `backend/main.py`:

1. **`on_close` handler** - Now properly logs connection closure:
   - Extracts `close_code` and `close_reason` from kwargs
   - Logs detailed info: `"Connection closed: code={code}, reason={reason}"`
   - Falls back to simple `"Connection closed"` message if no code

2. **`on_error` handler** - Now properly logs errors:
   - Extracts `error` from kwargs
   - Logs detailed info: `"Connection error: {error}"`  
   - Falls back to simple `"Connection error occurred"` message if no error details

3. **Verified registration order** - All 4 handlers are registered BEFORE `start_listening()`:
   ```python
   connection.on(EventType.OPEN, on_open)
   connection.on(EventType.MESSAGE, on_message)
   connection.on(EventType.CLOSE, on_close)
   connection.on(EventType.ERROR, on_error)
   # Start the event loop AFTER all handlers registered
   connection.start_listening()
   ```

### Commit
- Created commit `a0d47f4` with descriptive message
- Implementation plan updated to "completed" status
- Build progress updated with session 7 notes

The event handlers now properly follow the Deepgram SDK pattern with correct registration order and appropriate logging.

1â†’# Specification: Fix Git Merge & Configure Deepgram SDK
     2â†’
     3â†’## Overview
     4â†’
     5â†’This specification covers a two-phase critical task: (1) resolving a Git merge conflict where the current branch has no common history with `main`, requiring a force merge using `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` via terminal; and (2) implementing Epic 2: Connection & Configuration for a Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK integration in a greenfield voice AI assistant project. The project will use Python with the Deepgram SDK to establish real-time voice WebSocket connections.
     6â†’
     7â†’## Workflow Type
     8â†’
     9â†’**Type**: feature
    10â†’
    11â†’**Rationale**: This involves creating new functionality (Deepgram SDK integration) combined with a prerequisite infrastructure fix (Git merge). The task requires implementing new features rather than refactoring existing code or investigating issues.
    12â†’
    13â†’## Task Scope
    14â†’
    15â†’### Services Involved
    16â†’- **Voice Agent Backend** (primary/new) - Python service using Deepgram SDK for real-time voice processing
    17â†’- **Frontend Client** (future integration) - Will connect to backend for audio streaming (out of scope for this task)
    18â†’
    19â†’### This Task Will:
    20â†’- [ ] Execute `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` to force merge current branch into main via terminal
    21â†’- [ ] Resolve any merge conflicts that arise during the merge process
    22â†’- [ ] Install Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` Python package)
    23â†’- [ ] Create initial project structure (`/backend` directory with Python files)
    24â†’- [ ] Configure SDK settings with environment variables
    25â†’- [ ] Implement `keepAlive()` function to send KeepAlive control messages every 8 seconds
    26â†’- [ ] Establish WebSocket connection pattern using context manager
    27â†’
    28â†’### Out of Scope:
    29â†’- Frontend implementation (Epic 3)
    30â†’- Audio streaming and playback (Epic 3)
    31â†’- Interruption/barge-in logic (Epic 4)
    32â†’- SaaS features (auth, credits, Stripe) (Epic 5)
    33â†’- Deployment to Vercel/Render (Epic 6)
    34â†’
    35â†’## Service Context
    36â†’
    37â†’### Voice Agent Backend (New Service)
    38â†’
    39â†’**Tech Stack:**
    40â†’- Language: Python 3.10+
    41â†’- Framework: Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` v5.x)
    42â†’- Key directories: `/backend` (to be created)
    43â†’
    44â†’**Entry Point:** `backend/main.py` (to be created)
    45â†’
    46â†’**How to Run:**
    47â†’```bash
    48â†’# Install dependencies
    49â†’pip install deepgram-sdk python-dotenv
    50â†’
    51â†’# Run the application
    52â†’python backend/main.py
    53â†’```
    54â†’
    55â†’**Port:** TBD (not yet defined, will be determined during implementation)
    56â†’
    57â†’## Files to Create
    58â†’
    59â†’| File | Service | Purpose |
    60â†’|------|---------|---------|
    61â†’| `backend/main.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Main entry point with Deepgram connection setup |
    62â†’| `backend/requirements.txt` | Voice Agent Backend | Python dependencies (deepgram-sdk, python-dotenv) |
    63â†’| `backend/.env.example` | Voice Agent Backend | Environment variable template |
    64â†’| `backend/agent.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Agent configuration and keepAlive implementation |
    65â†’
    66â†’## Files to Reference
    67â†’
    68â†’Note: This is a greenfield project with no existing files to reference. The following patterns are derived from research and Deepgram SDK documentation:
    69â†’
    70â†’| Pattern | Source | What to Follow |
    71â†’|---------|--------|----------------|
    72â†’| Context manager pattern | Deepgram SDK docs | Use `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:` for automatic cleanup |
    73â†’| KeepAlive timing | Research findings (v5.x breaking change) | Send KeepAlive control message every 8 seconds, not 5 |
    74â†’| Event handler registration | Deepgram SDK docs | Register all handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()` |
    75â†’
    76â†’## Patterns to Follow
    77â†’
    78â†’### Deepgram Agent Connection Pattern
    79â†’
    80â†’From Deepgram SDK documentation:
    81â†’
    82â†’```python
    83â†’from deepgram import DeepgramClient
    84â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import (
    85â†’    AgentV1SettingsMessage, AgentV1ControlMessage, AgentV1MediaMessage
    86â†’)
    87â†’
    88â†’# Required: context manager pattern for automatic cleanup
    89â†’with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
    90â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    91â†’    connection.start_listening()
    92â†’```
    93â†’
    94â†’**Key Points:**
    95â†’- Context manager ensures proper connection cleanup on exit
    96â†’- Must send settings immediately after connection opens
    97â†’- `start_listening()` begins the event loop
    98â†’
    99â†’### KeepAlive Pattern (Critical - v5.x Breaking Change)
   100â†’
   101â†’```python
   102â†’import time
   103â†’
   104â†’def keep_alive_loop(connection):
   105â†’    """Send KeepAlive every 8 seconds during silence."""
   106â†’    while connection.is_connected:
   107â†’        connection.send_control(AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive'))
   108â†’        time.sleep(8)
   109â†’```
   110â†’
   111â†’**Key Points:**
   112â†’- KeepAlive is NOT automatic in v5.x (major breaking change)
   113â†’- Must be manually triggered every 8 seconds during silence periods
   114â†’- Server sends NO response to KeepAlive messages
   115â†’- Required to prevent WebSocket timeout during audio gaps
   116â†’
   117â†’### Event Handler Registration Pattern
   118â†’
   119â†’```python
   120â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
   121â†’    """Send settings when connection opens."""
   122â†’    settings = AgentV1SettingsMessage(
   123â†’        listen=AgentV1ListenSettings(model="nova-3"),
   124â†’        think=AgentV1ThinkSettings(provider="openai", model="gpt-4o-mini"),
   125â†’        speak=AgentV1SpeakSettings(voice="aura-2-thalia-en")
   126â†’    )
   127â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
   128â†’
   129â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
   130â†’    """Handle both JSON messages AND binary audio separately."""
   131â†’    # Check for message type
   132â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
   133â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
   134â†’        # JSON event handling
   135â†’        pass
   136â†’    else:
   137â†’        # Binary audio handling
   138â†’        pass
   139â†’
   140â†’# Register BEFORE start_listening()
   141â†’connection.on(LiveEvents.OPEN, on_open)
   142â†’connection.on(LiveEvents.MESSAGE, on_message)
   143â†’connection.start_listening()
   144â†’```
   145â†’
   146â†’**Key Points:**
   147â†’- Event handlers MUST be registered before `start_listening()`
   148â†’- `MESSAGE` event can receive both JSON and binary data
   149â†’- `OPEN` event is where settings should be sent
   150â†’
   151â†’## Requirements
   152â†’
   153â†’### Functional Requirements
   154â†’
   155â†’1. **Git Merge Resolution**
   156â†’   - Description: Force merge current branch into main using terminal Git commands
   157â†’   - Acceptance: Merge completes successfully, `git log` shows merged commits
   158â†’
   159â†’2. **Deepgram SDK Installation**
   160â†’   - Description: Install `deepgram-sdk` v5.x and `python-dotenv` packages
   161â†’   - Acceptance: `pip show deepgram-sdk` shows installed package
   162â†’
   163â†’3. **Project Structure**
   164â†’   - Description: Create `/backend` directory with initial Python files
   165â†’   - Acceptance: Directory exists with `main.py`, `requirements.txt`, `.env.example`, `agent.py`
   166â†’
   167â†’4. **Environment Configuration**
   168â†’   - Description: Configure DEEPGRAM_API_KEY and optionally OPENAI_API_KEY
   169â†’   - Acceptance: `.env` file exists with required variables, `.env.example` documents them
   170â†’
   171â†’5. **WebSocket Connection**
   172â†’   - Description: Establish connection using context manager pattern
   173â†’   - Acceptance: Connection opens successfully, OPEN event fires
   174â†’
   175â†’6. **Agent Settings Configuration**
   176â†’   - Description: Configure Listen (nova-3), Think (gpt-4o-mini), Speak (aura-2-thalia-en)
   177â†’   - Acceptance: Settings sent successfully after connection opens
   178â†’
   179â†’7. **KeepAlive Function**
   180â†’   - Description: Implement keepAlive() sending control message every 8 seconds
   181â†’   - Acceptance: Function sends `AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive')` every 8s during silence
   182â†’
   183â†’### Edge Cases
   184â†’
   185â†’1. **Git Merge Conflicts** - Resolve any conflicts manually, preserving both histories where possible
   186â†’2. **Missing API Keys** - Raise clear error message if DEEPGRAM_API_KEY not set
   187â†’3. **Connection Failure** - Implement retry logic or graceful error handling
   188â†’4. **KeepAlive During Audio** - Only send KeepAlive during silence periods, not during active transmission
   189â†’5. **WebSocket Disconnect** - Handle CLOSE and ERROR events with cleanup
   190â†’
   191â†’## Implementation Notes
   192â†’
   193â†’### DO
   194â†’- Use terminal for Git merge: `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories <branch-name>`
   195â†’- Create `/backend` directory structure following Python best practices
   196â†’- Use context manager pattern: `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:`

   199â†’- Use python-dotenv for environment variable management
   200â†’- Include `.env.example` in version control (not `.env`)
   201â†’
   202â†’### DON'T
   203â†’- Do NOT use GitHub PR web interface for the merge (will fail with unrelated histories)
   204â†’- Do NOT send KeepAlive every 5 seconds (research confirms 8 seconds is correct)
   205â†’- Do NOT call `start_listening()` before registering event handlers
   206â†’- Do NOT commit `.env` file with actual API keys
   207â†’- Do NOT implement frontend features (Epic 3) in this task
   208â†’
   209â†’### Git Merge Specific Steps

1â†’"""
     2â†’Deepgram Voice Agent Configuration Module
     3â†’
     4â†’This module provides agent settings and configuration for the Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK.
     5â†’"""
     6â†’
     7â†’import time
     8â†’import threading
     9â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import AgentV1SettingsMessage, AgentV1ControlMessage
    10â†’
    11â†’
    12â†’def get_agent_settings():
    13â†’    """
    14â†’    Create and return Deepgram Agent V1 settings.
    15â†’
    16â†’    Returns:
    17â†’        AgentV1SettingsMessage: Configured agent settings with:
    18â†’            - Listen: Uses Deepgram's nova-3 model with smart formatting
    19â†’            - Think: Uses OpenAI's gpt-4o-mini model
    20â†’            - Speak: Uses Deepgram's aura-2-thalia-en voice
    21â†’            - Greeting: Initial welcome message
    22â†’
    23â†’    Example:
    24â†’        >>> settings = get_agent_settings()
    25â†’        >>> connection.send_settings(settings)
    26â†’    """
    27â†’    settings = AgentV1SettingsMessage(
    28â†’        listen={
    29â†’            "provider": {
    30â†’                "type": "deepgram",
    31â†’                "model": "nova-3"
    32â†’            },
    33â†’            "smart_format": True
    34â†’        },
    35â†’        think={
    36â†’            "provider": {
    37â†’                "type": "open_ai",
    38â†’                "model": "gpt-4o-mini"
    39â†’            },
    40â†’            "prompt": "You are a friendly AI assistant.",
    41â†’            "temperature": 0.7
    42â†’        },
    43â†’        speak={
    44â†’            "provider": {
    45â†’                "type": "deepgram",
    46â†’                "model": "aura-2-thalia-en"
    47â†’            }
    48â†’        },
    49â†’        greeting="Hello! How can I help you today?"
    50â†’    )
    51â†’    return settings
    52â†’
    53â†’
    54â†’def keep_alive_loop(connection, stop_event, interval=8):
    55â†’    """
    56â†’    Send KeepAlive control messages periodically to maintain WebSocket connection.
    57â†’
    58â†’    This function runs in a separate thread and sends KeepAlive messages every
    59â†’    8 seconds (by default) to prevent the WebSocket connection from timing out
    60â†’    during periods of silence or inactivity.
    61â†’
    62â†’    Args:
    63â†’        connection: Deepgram Agent V1 connection object
    64â†’        stop_event (threading.Event): Event to signal when to stop the loop
    65â†’        interval (int, optional): Seconds between KeepAlive messages. Defaults to 8.
    66â†’
    67â†’    Note:
    68â†’        - KeepAlive is NOT automatic in Deepgram SDK v5.x (breaking change)
    69â†’        - Server sends NO response to KeepAlive messages
    70â†’        - Only send during silence periods, not during active audio transmission
    71â†’
    72â†’    Example:
    73â†’        >>> stop_event = threading.Event()
    74â†’        >>> thread = threading.Thread(
    75â†’        ...     target=keep_alive_loop,
    76â†’        ...     args=(connection, stop_event)
    77â†’        ... )
    78â†’        >>> thread.start()
    79â†’        >>> # ... later when connection closes ...
    80â†’        >>> stop_event.set()
    81â†’        >>> thread.join()
    82â†’    """
    83â†’    while not stop_event.is_set():
    84â†’        try:
    85â†’            connection.send_control(AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive'))
    86â†’            time.sleep(interval)
    87â†’        except Exception as e:
    88â†’            # Connection may be closed or error occurred
    89â†’            # Exit loop to prevent continuous errors
    90â†’            break
    91â†’

## Subtask 5-1 Completed âś…

Successfully implemented the `keep_alive_loop()` function in `backend/agent.py`.

### Implementation Summary

**Added imports:**
- `time` - for the sleep interval
- `threading` - for the stop_event threading primitive
- `AgentV1ControlMessage` - for sending KeepAlive control messages

**Function signature:**
```python
def keep_alive_loop(connection, stop_event, interval=8):
```

**Key features:**
- âś… Sends `AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive')` every **8 seconds** (critical v5.x breaking change)
- âś… Uses `threading.Event` for graceful shutdown
- âś… Includes error handling to exit loop on connection failures
- âś… Comprehensive docstring with usage examples
- âś… Follows existing code style and patterns

**Quality checklist:**
- [x] Follows patterns from reference files
- [x] No debugging print statements
- [x] Error handling in place
- [x] Correct 8-second interval (not 5)
- [x] Clean commit (603974b)

### Next Step

**Subtask 5-2:** Integrate keepAlive into main.py connection loop
- Start `keep_alive_loop` thread in OPEN handler
- Signal `stop_event` when connection closes (CLOSE handler)
I'll help you integrate keepAlive into main.py. Let me start by understanding the current codebase structure and any existing keepAlive implementation.
Let me check the agent.py file to see the keep_alive_loop implementation:

entries.detail:
1â†’"""
     2â†’Deepgram Voice Agent Configuration Module
     3â†’
     4â†’This module provides agent settings and configuration for the Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK.
     5â†’"""
     6â†’
     7â†’import time
     8â†’import threading
     9â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import AgentV1SettingsMessage, AgentV1ControlMessage
    10â†’
    11â†’
    12â†’def get_agent_settings():
    13â†’    """
    14â†’    Create and return Deepgram Agent V1 settings.
    15â†’
    16â†’    Returns:
    17â†’        AgentV1SettingsMessage: Configured agent settings with:
    18â†’            - Listen: Uses Deepgram's nova-3 model with smart formatting
    19â†’            - Think: Uses OpenAI's gpt-4o-mini model
    20â†’            - Speak: Uses Deepgram's aura-2-thalia-en voice
    21â†’            - Greeting: Initial welcome message
    22â†’
    23â†’    Example:
    24â†’        >>> settings = get_agent_settings()
    25â†’        >>> connection.send_settings(settings)
    26â†’    """
    27â†’    settings = AgentV1SettingsMessage(
    28â†’        listen={
    29â†’            "provider": {
    30â†’                "type": "deepgram",
    31â†’                "model": "nova-3"
    32â†’            },
    33â†’            "smart_format": True
    34â†’        },
    35â†’        think={
    36â†’            "provider": {
    37â†’                "type": "open_ai",
    38â†’                "model": "gpt-4o-mini"
    39â†’            },
    40â†’            "prompt": "You are a friendly AI assistant.",
    41â†’            "temperature": 0.7
    42â†’        },
    43â†’        speak={
    44â†’            "provider": {
    45â†’                "type": "deepgram",
    46â†’                "model": "aura-2-thalia-en"
    47â†’            }
    48â†’        },
    49â†’        greeting="Hello! How can I help you today?"
    50â†’    )
    51â†’    return settings
    52â†’
    53â†’
    54â†’def keep_alive_loop(connection, stop_event, interval=8):
    55â†’    """
    56â†’    Send KeepAlive control messages periodically to maintain WebSocket connection.
    57â†’
    58â†’    This function runs in a separate thread and sends KeepAlive messages every
    59â†’    8 seconds (by default) to prevent the WebSocket connection from timing out
    60â†’    during periods of silence or inactivity.
    61â†’
    62â†’    Args:
    63â†’        connection: Deepgram Agent V1 connection object
    64â†’        stop_event (threading.Event): Event to signal when to stop the loop
    65â†’        interval (int, optional): Seconds between KeepAlive messages. Defaults to 8.
    66â†’
    67â†’    Note:
    68â†’        - KeepAlive is NOT automatic in Deepgram SDK v5.x (breaking change)
    69â†’        - Server sends NO response to KeepAlive messages
    70â†’        - Only send during silence periods, not during active audio transmission
    71â†’
    72â†’    Example:
    73â†’        >>> stop_event = threading.Event()
    74â†’        >>> thread = threading.Thread(
    75â†’        ...     target=keep_alive_loop,
    76â†’        ...     args=(connection, stop_event)
    77â†’        ... )
    78â†’        >>> thread.start()
    79â†’        >>> # ... later when connection closes ...
    80â†’        >>> stop_event.set()
    81â†’        >>> thread.join()
    82â†’    """
    83â†’    while not stop_event.is_set():
    84â†’        try:
    85â†’            connection.send_control(AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive'))
    86â†’            time.sleep(interval)
    87â†’        except Exception as e:
    88â†’            # Connection may be closed or error occurred
    89â†’            # Exit loop to prevent continuous errors
    90â†’            break
    91â†’

1â†’"""
     2â†’Deepgram Voice Agent Main Entry Point
     3â†’
     4â†’This module initializes the Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK with environment
     5â†’configuration and establishes WebSocket connections for real-time voice processing.
     6â†’"""
     7â†’
     8â†’import os
     9â†’from dotenv import load_dotenv
    10â†’from deepgram import DeepgramClient
    11â†’from deepgram.core.events import EventType
    12â†’from deepgram.extensions.types.sockets import AgentV1ControlMessage
    13â†’
    14â†’from agent import get_agent_settings
    15â†’
    16â†’
    17â†’def initialize_deepgram_client():
    18â†’    """
    19â†’    Initialize and return a DeepgramClient instance with API key from environment.
    20â†’
    21â†’    Returns:
    22â†’        DeepgramClient: Configured Deepgram client instance
    23â†’
    24â†’    Raises:
    25â†’        ValueError: If DEEPGRAM_API_KEY is not set in environment variables
    26â†’
    27â†’    Example:
    28â†’        >>> client = initialize_deepgram_client()
    29â†’        >>> with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
    30â†’        ...     # Handle connection
    31â†’    """
    32â†’    # Load environment variables from .env file
    33â†’    load_dotenv()
    34â†’
    35â†’    # Get API key from environment
    36â†’    deepgram_api_key = os.getenv("DEEPGRAM_API_KEY")
    37â†’
    38â†’    # Validate API key is present
    39â†’    if not deepgram_api_key:
    40â†’        raise ValueError(
    41â†’            "DEEPGRAM_API_KEY environment variable is not set. "
    42â†’            "Please set it in your .env file or environment."
    43â†’        )
    44â†’
    45â†’    # Create and return DeepgramClient instance
    46â†’    return DeepgramClient(deepgram_api_key)
    47â†’
    48â†’
    49â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
    50â†’    """
    51â†’    Handle the WebSocket OPEN event.
    52â†’
    53â†’    This event fires when the connection is established. Send agent settings
    54â†’    immediately after connection opens to configure the voice agent.
    55â†’
    56â†’    Args:
    57â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    58â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments
    59â†’    """
    60â†’    settings = get_agent_settings()
    61â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    62â†’
    63â†’
    64â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
    65â†’    """
    66â†’    Handle the WebSocket MESSAGE event.
    67â†’
    68â†’    This event receives both JSON control messages and binary audio data.
    69â†’    Check message type to determine handling approach.
    70â†’
    71â†’    Args:
    72â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    73â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'message' which may be
    74â†’                  a dict (JSON) or bytes (binary audio)
    75â†’    """
    76â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
    77â†’
    78â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
    79â†’        # Handle JSON control messages
    80â†’        # TODO: Implement JSON message handling in future subtasks
    81â†’        pass
    82â†’    elif isinstance(message, bytes):
    83â†’        # Handle binary audio data
    84â†’        # TODO: Implement audio handling in future subtasks
    85â†’        pass
    86â†’
    87â†’
    88â†’def on_close(connection, **kwargs):
    89â†’    """
    90â†’    Handle the WebSocket CLOSE event.
    91â†’
    92â†’    This event fires when the connection is closed. Log the closure
    93â†’    and perform any necessary cleanup.
    94â†’
    95â†’    Args:
    96â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    97â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'code' (close code)
    98â†’                  and 'reason' (close reason)
    99â†’    """
   100â†’    close_code = kwargs.get("code")
   101â†’    close_reason = kwargs.get("reason")
   102â†’
   103â†’    if close_code:
   104â†’        print(f"Connection closed: code={close_code}, reason={close_reason}")
   105â†’    else:
   106â†’        print("Connection closed")
   107â†’
   108â†’
   109â†’def on_error(connection, **kwargs):
   110â†’    """
   111â†’    Handle the WebSocket ERROR event.
   112â†’
   113â†’    This event fires when an error occurs on the connection.
   114â†’
   115â†’    Args:
   116â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
   117â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'error' with error details
   118â†’    """
   119â†’    error = kwargs.get("error")
   120â†’    if error:
   121â†’        print(f"Connection error: {error}")
   122â†’    else:
   123â†’        print("Connection error occurred")
   124â†’
   125â†’
   126â†’def main():
   127â†’    """
   128â†’    Main entry point for the Deepgram Voice Agent application.
   129â†’
   130â†’    Establishes WebSocket connection using context manager pattern,
   131â†’    registers event handlers, and starts listening for voice events.
   132â†’    """
   133â†’    try:
   134â†’        # Initialize the Deepgram client with environment configuration
   135â†’        client = initialize_deepgram_client()
   136â†’
   137â†’        # Use context manager for automatic connection cleanup
   138â†’        with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
   139â†’            # Register event handlers BEFORE calling start_listening()
   140â†’            connection.on(EventType.OPEN, on_open)
   141â†’            connection.on(EventType.MESSAGE, on_message)
   142â†’            connection.on(EventType.CLOSE, on_close)
   143â†’            connection.on(EventType.ERROR, on_error)
   144â†’
   145â†’            # Start the event loop
   146â†’            connection.start_listening()
   147â†’
   148â†’    except ValueError as e:
   149â†’        print(f"Configuration error: {e}")
   150â†’        return 1
   151â†’    except Exception as e:
   152â†’        print(f"Unexpected error: {e}")
   153â†’        return 1
   154â†’
   155â†’    return 0
   156â†’
   157â†’
   158â†’if __name__ == "__main__":
   159â†’    exit(main())
   160â†’

     5â†’configuration and establishes WebSocket connections for real-time voice processing.

    18â†’def initialize_deepgram_client():
    19â†’    """

    21â†’

    23â†’        DeepgramClient: Configured Deepgram client instance

    25â†’    Raises:
    26â†’        ValueError: If DEEPGRAM_API_KEY is not set in environment variables
    27â†’
    28â†’    Example:

    31â†’        ...     # Handle connection
    32â†’    """
    33â†’    # Load environment variables from .env file
    34â†’    load_dotenv()
    35â†’
    36â†’    # Get API key from environment
    37â†’    deepgram_api_key = os.getenv("DEEPGRAM_API_KEY")
    38â†’
    39â†’    # Validate API key is present

    41â†’        raise ValueError(

    43â†’            "Please set it in your .env file or environment."
    44â†’        )
    45â†’
    46â†’    # Create and return DeepgramClient instance
    47â†’    return DeepgramClient(deepgram_api_key)
    48â†’
    49â†’
    50â†’def on_open(connection, **kwargs):
    51â†’    """
    52â†’    Handle the WebSocket OPEN event.
    53â†’
    54â†’    This event fires when the connection is established. Send agent settings
    55â†’    immediately after connection opens to configure the voice agent.
    56â†’    Also starts the keep_alive_loop thread to maintain the connection.
    57â†’
    58â†’    Args:
    59â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
    60â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments
    61â†’    """
    62â†’    settings = get_agent_settings()
    63â†’    connection.send_settings(settings)
    64â†’
    65â†’    # Start keep_alive_loop in a separate thread
    66â†’    # Store thread and stop_event in connection object for cleanup in on_close
    67â†’    connection._keep_alive_stop = threading.Event()
    68â†’    connection._keep_alive_thread = threading.Thread(
    69â†’        target=keep_alive_loop,
    70â†’        args=(connection, connection._keep_alive_stop),
    71â†’        daemon=True
    72â†’    )
    73â†’    connection._keep_alive_thread.start()
    74â†’
    75â†’
    76â†’def on_message(connection, **kwargs):
    77â†’    """
    78â†’    Handle the WebSocket MESSAGE event.

    82â†’
    83â†’    Args:

    86â†’                  a dict (JSON) or bytes (binary audio)
    87â†’    """
    88â†’    message = kwargs.get("message")
    89â†’
    90â†’    if isinstance(message, dict):
    91â†’        # Handle JSON control messages
    92â†’        # TODO: Implement JSON message handling in future subtasks
    93â†’        pass
    94â†’    elif isinstance(message, bytes):
    95â†’        # Handle binary audio data
    96â†’        # TODO: Implement audio handling in future subtasks
    97â†’        pass
    98â†’
    99â†’
   100â†’def on_close(connection, **kwargs):
   101â†’    """
   102â†’    Handle the WebSocket CLOSE event.
   103â†’
   104â†’    This event fires when the connection is closed. Log the closure,
   105â†’    stop the keep_alive_loop thread, and perform any necessary cleanup.
   106â†’
   107â†’    Args:
   108â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
   109â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'code' (close code)
   110â†’                  and 'reason' (close reason)

   124â†’    else:
   125â†’        print("Connection closed")

   127â†’
   128â†’def on_error(connection, **kwargs):
   129â†’    """
   130â†’    Handle the WebSocket ERROR event.
   131â†’
   132â†’    This event fires when an error occurs on the connection.
   133â†’
   134â†’    Args:
   135â†’        connection: The Deepgram agent connection object
   136â†’        **kwargs: Additional event arguments including 'error' with error details
   137â†’    """
   138â†’    error = kwargs.get("error")
   139â†’    if error:
   140â†’        print(f"Connection error: {error}")
   141â†’    else:

   143â†’
   144â†’

   146â†’    """

   148â†’
   149â†’    Establishes WebSocket connection using context manager pattern,
   150â†’    registers event handlers, and starts listening for voice events.
   151â†’    """
   152â†’    try:
   153â†’        # Initialize the Deepgram client with environment configuration
   154â†’        client = initialize_deepgram_client()
   155â†’
   156â†’        # Use context manager for automatic connection cleanup
   157â†’        with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:
   158â†’            # Register event handlers BEFORE calling start_listening()
   159â†’            connection.on(EventType.OPEN, on_open)
   160â†’            connection.on(EventType.MESSAGE, on_message)
   161â†’            connection.on(EventType.CLOSE, on_close)
   162â†’            connection.on(EventType.ERROR, on_error)
   163â†’
   164â†’            # Start the event loop

   167â†’    except ValueError as e:

1â†’# Specification: Fix Git Merge & Configure Deepgram SDK
     2â†’
     3â†’## Overview
     4â†’
     5â†’This specification covers a two-phase critical task: (1) resolving a Git merge conflict where the current branch has no common history with `main`, requiring a force merge using `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` via terminal; and (2) implementing Epic 2: Connection & Configuration for a Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK integration in a greenfield voice AI assistant project. The project will use Python with the Deepgram SDK to establish real-time voice WebSocket connections.
     6â†’
     7â†’## Workflow Type
     8â†’
     9â†’**Type**: feature
    10â†’
    11â†’**Rationale**: This involves creating new functionality (Deepgram SDK integration) combined with a prerequisite infrastructure fix (Git merge). The task requires implementing new features rather than refactoring existing code or investigating issues.
    12â†’
    13â†’## Task Scope
    14â†’
    15â†’### Services Involved
    16â†’- **Voice Agent Backend** (primary/new) - Python service using Deepgram SDK for real-time voice processing
    17â†’- **Frontend Client** (future integration) - Will connect to backend for audio streaming (out of scope for this task)
    18â†’
    19â†’### This Task Will:
    20â†’- [ ] Execute `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` to force merge current branch into main via terminal
    21â†’- [ ] Resolve any merge conflicts that arise during the merge process
    22â†’- [ ] Install Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` Python package)
    23â†’- [ ] Create initial project structure (`/backend` directory with Python files)
    24â†’- [ ] Configure SDK settings with environment variables
    25â†’- [ ] Implement `keepAlive()` function to send KeepAlive control messages every 8 seconds
    26â†’- [ ] Establish WebSocket connection pattern using context manager
    27â†’
    28â†’### Out of Scope:
    29â†’- Frontend implementation (Epic 3)
    30â†’- Audio streaming and playback (Epic 3)
    31â†’- Interruption/barge-in logic (Epic 4)
    32â†’- SaaS features (auth, credits, Stripe) (Epic 5)
    33â†’- Deployment to Vercel/Render (Epic 6)
    34â†’
    35â†’## Service Context
    36â†’
    37â†’### Voice Agent Backend (New Service)
    38â†’
    39â†’**Tech Stack:**
    40â†’- Language: Python 3.10+
    41â†’- Framework: Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK (`deepgram-sdk` v5.x)
    42â†’- Key directories: `/backend` (to be created)
    43â†’
    44â†’**Entry Point:** `backend/main.py` (to be created)
    45â†’
    46â†’**How to Run:**
    47â†’```bash
    48â†’# Install dependencies
    49â†’pip install deepgram-sdk python-dotenv
    50â†’
    51â†’# Run the application
    52â†’python backend/main.py
    53â†’```
    54â†’
    55â†’**Port:** TBD (not yet defined, will be determined during implementation)
    56â†’
    57â†’## Files to Create
    58â†’
    59â†’| File | Service | Purpose |
    60â†’|------|---------|---------|
    61â†’| `backend/main.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Main entry point with Deepgram connection setup |
    62â†’| `backend/requirements.txt` | Voice Agent Backend | Python dependencies (deepgram-sdk, python-dotenv) |
    63â†’| `backend/.env.example` | Voice Agent Backend | Environment variable template |
    64â†’| `backend/agent.py` | Voice Agent Backend | Agent configuration and keepAlive implementation |
    65â†’
    66â†’## Files to Reference
    67â†’
    68â†’Note: This is a greenfield project with no existing files to reference. The following patterns are derived from research and Deepgram SDK documentation:
    69â†’
    70â†’| Pattern | Source | What to Follow |
    71â†’|---------|--------|----------------|
    72â†’| Context manager pattern | Deepgram SDK docs | Use `with client.agent.v1.connect() as connection:` for automatic cleanup |
    73â†’| KeepAlive timing | Research findings (v5.x breaking change) | Send KeepAlive control message every 8 seconds, not 5 |
    74â†’| Event handler registration | Deepgram SDK docs | Register all handlers BEFORE calling `start_listening()` |
    75â†’
    76â†’## Patterns to Follow
    77â†’
    78â†’### Deepgram Agent Connection Pattern
    79â†’
    80â†’From Deepgram SDK documentation:
    81â†’
    82â†’```python
    83â†’from deepgram import DeepgramClient

    99â†’### KeepAlive Pattern (Critical - v5.x Breaking Change)

   117â†’### Event Handler Registration Pattern

   146â†’**Key Points:**

   148â†’- `MESSAGE` event can receive both JSON and binary data

     4â†’This module provides agent settings and configuration for the Deepgram Voice Agent V1 SDK.

    10â†’

    12â†’def get_agent_settings():
    13â†’    """
    14â†’    Create and return Deepgram Agent V1 settings.
    15â†’
    16â†’    Returns:
    17â†’        AgentV1SettingsMessage: Configured agent settings with:
    18â†’            - Listen: Uses Deepgram's nova-3 model with smart formatting
    19â†’            - Think: Uses OpenAI's gpt-4o-mini model
    20â†’            - Speak: Uses Deepgram's aura-2-thalia-en voice

    22â†’
    23â†’    Example:
    24â†’        >>> settings = get_agent_settings()
    25â†’        >>> connection.send_settings(settings)
    26â†’    """
    27â†’    settings = AgentV1SettingsMessage(
    28â†’        listen={
    29â†’            "provider": {
    30â†’                "type": "deepgram",
    31â†’                "model": "nova-3"
    32â†’            },
    33â†’            "smart_format": True
    34â†’        },
    35â†’        think={
    36â†’            "provider": {

    38â†’                "model": "gpt-4o-mini"
    39â†’            },
    40â†’            "prompt": "You are a friendly AI assistant.",
    41â†’            "temperature": 0.7
    42â†’        },
    43â†’        speak={

    47â†’            }

    54â†’def keep_alive_loop(connection, stop_event, interval=8):
    55â†’    """
    56â†’    Send KeepAlive control messages periodically to maintain WebSocket connection.
    57â†’
    58â†’    This function runs in a separate thread and sends KeepAlive messages every
    59â†’    8 seconds (by default) to prevent the WebSocket connection from timing out
    60â†’    during periods of silence or inactivity.
    61â†’
    62â†’    Args:
    63â†’        connection: Deepgram Agent V1 connection object
    64â†’        stop_event (threading.Event): Event to signal when to stop the loop
    65â†’        interval (int, optional): Seconds between KeepAlive messages. Defaults to 8.
    66â†’
    67â†’    Note:
    68â†’        - KeepAlive is NOT automatic in Deepgram SDK v5.x (breaking change)
    69â†’        - Server sends NO response to KeepAlive messages
    70â†’        - Only send during silence periods, not during active audio transmission
    71â†’
    72â†’    Example:
    73â†’        >>> stop_event = threading.Event()
    74â†’        >>> thread = threading.Thread(
    75â†’        ...     target=keep_alive_loop,
    76â†’        ...     args=(connection, stop_event)
    77â†’        ... )
    78â†’        >>> thread.start()
    79â†’        >>> # ... later when connection closes ...
    80â†’        >>> stop_event.set()
    81â†’        >>> thread.join()
    82â†’    """
    83â†’    while not stop_event.is_set():
    84â†’        try:
    85â†’            connection.send_control(AgentV1ControlMessage(type='KeepAlive'))
    86â†’            time.sleep(interval)
    87â†’        except Exception as e:
    88â†’            # Connection may be closed or error occurred
    89â†’            # Exit loop to prevent continuous errors
    90â†’            break
